methodology_note: This comparison uses only measurable evidence from the actual texts. Sunnah.com indexes 9 primary + 9 secondary Sunni collections only — a hadith not found there may exist in other legitimate Sunni works (e.g., al-Mustadrak, al-Tabarani, Hilyat al-Awliya, Tarikh Baghdad). "NOT_FOUND" means absent from Sunnah.com's indexed books, not from all Sunni literature. The comparison is limited to one passage from Dehlavi (~80 lines) vs one volume from Mir Hamid Husain (~17,690 lines) — a broader study across all volumes would be needed for a complete assessment.

citation_practice.assessment: Quotes hadith texts verbatim without naming any source books; Sunnah.com later identifies collections (e.g., Sahih Muslim), indicating reliance on memorized or intermediary transmission.

methodological_transparency.assessment: No chains or collection names provided; logical structures explicit (e.g., reductio ad absurdum), but interpretive assumptions unstated.

grade_accuracy.assessment: Claims 'sahih' for multiple hadiths without naming authenticators. Of verifiable claims: Thaqalayn confirmed sahih (Sahih Muslim); Rashidun Sunnah hadith confirmed (Sunan collections); Aisha 'half your religion' hadith declared fabricated (mawdu') by al-Iraqi, al-Sakhawi, al-Albani; Abu Bakr/Umar hadith graded Hasan only (Tirmidhi) — Dehlavi claims 'tawatur ma'nawi' without evidence. Safinah and Shura hadiths not found in Sunnah.com's 18 indexed collections — this does not mean fabricated, but Dehlavi provides no source references to verify them elsewhere.

engagement.assessment: Minimizes Thaqalayn via interpretation and counters with unverified hadiths; ignores Shi'a dalalah proofs; no direct citation of opposing sources.

scope.assessment: Narrow: 4 hadiths across 4 arguments; no named sources, no biographical or geographic metrics discernible.

internal_consistency.assessment: Consistent minimization strategy (meaning, counter-hadith, logic); no internal contradictions in provided arguments.

dehlavi.overall_scholarly_grade: D: Polemical brevity with unverified citations and absent source transparency undermines rigor.

citation_practice.assessment: Names 197 unique Sunni books explicitly; cites primary compilers (e.g., al-Shafi'i, al-Tabarani, al-Hakim) with companions; distinguishes primary (e.g., Mustadrak) from secondary.

methodological_transparency.assessment: Provides isnads for all 40 narrator citations; names collections/books per tradition; structures dalalah proofs in 3 numbered sections with explicit reasoning.

grade_accuracy.grading_claims_made: N/A (focus on transmission, not explicit grading)
grade_accuracy.assessment: No direct 'sahih' claims in data; relies on early Sunni authorities (d.204-427 AH) and biographical evaluations from rijal works (e.g., Fath al-Bari), verifiable via named sources.

engagement.assessment: Directly refutes Dehlavi by section (e.g., 3,44-45,59-60); engages minimization via dalalah proofs and source expansion beyond Sunnah.com scope.

scope.time_span: 204-427 AH (earliest: al-Shafi'i; latest: Ibn Mardawayh al-Qattani)
scope.assessment: Expansive: 54 unique scholars, 40 narrator dossiers with companions (e.g., Abu Dharr, Ibn Abbas); geographic diversity inferred (Nishapur, Baghdad, Tabaran via authors); 10+ biographical evaluations.

internal_consistency.assessment: Uniform methodology: Sunni source citation, isnad provision, sectional dalalah arguments consistent across Vol 23 responses.

mir_hamid_husain.overall_scholarly_grade: A: Exceptional source depth, transparency, and engagement with verifiable Sunni texts beyond standard collections.

key_differences: Dehlavi: 0 named books vs. Husain: 197 unique books
Dehlavi: 50% grading claims fabricated/unverified vs. Husain: no grading claims, focuses on transmission evidence
Dehlavi: no isnads/collections vs. Husain: full isnads + named collections for 40 narrators
Dehlavi: 4 arguments, bare quotes vs. Husain: 3 dalalah sections with 54 scholars
Dehlavi: time span N/A vs. Husain: 223-year span (204-427 AH)
Dehlavi: no biographical rigor vs. Husain: 5+ dossiers with rijal sources
Dehlavi: unstated assumptions vs. Husain: numbered reasoning steps
comparative_summary.what_this_tells_us: Dehlavi employs concise polemical interpretation with untraced hadiths; Husain demonstrates comprehensive source-critical methodology, prioritizing transmission documentation from early Sunni literature to substantiate dalalah, aligning with standards of hadith scholarship.
